g.text: Single cuts heal because the UniC corrector rebuilds the state from the OTHER channel

text.style: font-size: 9.5px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans', 'Bitstream Vera Sans', 'Computer Modern Sans Serif', 'Lucida Grande', 'Verdana', 'Geneva', 'Lucid', 'Arial', 'Helvetica', 'Avant Garde', sans-serif; fill: #52514e

g.text: (weight 0.94-0.97). The joint cut (both channels) is 4-15x worse — conditioning re-

text.style: font-size: 9.5px; font-family: 'DejaVu Sans', 'Bitstream Vera Sans', 'Computer Modern Sans Serif', 'Lucida Grande', 'Verdana', 'Geneva', 'Lucid', 'Arial', 'Helvetica', 'Avant Garde', sans-serif; fill: #52514e

g.text: derives only about half. With x0-hat kept, zeroing history stays cheap: content rides

g.text: Cross-step memory is a redundant pair: x_t content + corrector anchor
